food_subgroup: Battered and fried seafood with egg-based sauce coating

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for salted egg yolk coated fried prawn specifically; closest parent 'shrimp food product' (FOODON_03302051) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Shrimps and prawns, cooked) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for salted egg yolk battered fried prawn preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227144002 'Prawn (substance)' is the closest parent; no SNOMED code exists for the specific salted egg yolk preparation.

preparation_method: wok-tossed in salted egg yolk sauce
coated with curry leaf and chilli butter sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of crispy salted egg yolk prawn is approximately 250 g, representing a shared portion of 4–6 pieces of whole prawns coated in golden salted egg yolk sauce, suitable for 2–3 diners as part of a multi-dish Chinese meal.

nutrient_source.title: Fried Prawn / Shrimp with Egg Coating — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Shrimp, cooked, NDB 15152) and USDA Foundation Foods (Egg yolk, raw, NDB 01125) combined with standard deep-frying fat absorption data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for crispy salted egg yolk prawn exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA SR Legacy cooked shrimp (NDB 15152) as the protein and mineral base; (2) USDA Foundation Foods egg yolk (NDB 01125) for additional fat, cholesterol, and micronutrients contributed by salted duck egg yolk; (3) standard deep-frying fat absorption factor of approximately 8–12% added fat by weight for battered seafood (FAO/INFOODS methodology); (4) butter and evaporated milk contribution to saturated fat and sodium. The resulting composite reflects a typical restaurant-style preparation. Values are conservative mid-range estimates. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of battered fried seafood — analogous estimate based on 'Battered fish' and 'Fried shrimp' entries; University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for crispy salted egg yolk prawn. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous battered fried seafood entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) International Tables of GI and GL Values. The cornstarch-based batter contributes moderate glycemic carbohydrates, but the high protein and fat content of the dish suppresses the overall glycemic response. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: (45 × 9.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2.5) / 100 = approximately 11.0 g GL.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from salted duck egg yolk, butter, and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 520 mg sodium per 100 g (up to 1300 mg per 250 g serving), this dish contributes a substantial proportion of the 2000 mg daily sodium limit recommended for hypertensive individuals and should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from cornstarch batter, partially offset by high protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low GI of approximately 45 and a glycemic load of around 11 per serving, this dish has a moderate glycemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, pair with non-starchy vegetables, and account for the high saturated fat from butter and egg yolk which may worsen insulin resistance over time.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat and cholesterol from salted duck egg yolk and butter, combined with deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of deep-frying, butter, and salted duck egg yolk results in approximately 5.2 g saturated fat and 185 mg cholesterol per 100 g, making this dish a high cardiovascular risk food that should be limited to occasional consumption by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crispy Salted Egg Yolk Prawn (香酥蛋黄虾) is a popular Chinese-Singaporean restaurant dish featuring whole prawns that are coated in a light cornstarch batter, deep-fried until golden and crunchy, then wok-tossed in a rich, aromatic sauce made from mashed salted duck egg yolks, butter, evaporated milk, curry leaves, and fresh chilli. The result is a dish with a distinctively crispy exterior, juicy and tender prawn flesh, and a deeply savory, buttery, slightly spicy coating with the characteristic grainy, golden texture of salted egg yolk. The dish is a hallmark of the salted egg yolk craze that swept Singapore and Southeast Asia in the 2010s and remains a staple on Chinese restaurant and zi char menus. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (14.5 g), significant fat (13.8 g, of which 5.2 g is saturated), and moderate carbohydrate from the batter (9.8 g). Sodium content is high at approximately 520 mg per 100 g due to the salted egg yolk and seasoning. Cholesterol is elevated at approximately 185 mg per 100 g. The dish is not suitable for low-sodium or low-cholesterol diets and should be consumed in moderation by individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes. It contains crustacean, egg, dairy, and gluten allergens. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low category), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 11, and the high saturated fat content may impair insulin sensitivity with regular consumption.
common_user_queries: What is crispy salted egg yolk prawn?

Is salted egg yolk prawn healthy?

Is salted egg yolk prawn halal?
How is salted egg yolk prawn made?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for crispy salted egg yolk prawn was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cooked shrimp, USDA Foundation Foods egg yolk, and standard deep-frying fat absorption methodology. GI is an analogous estimate from battered fried seafood entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level sources are high-quality approved databases and the estimation methodology is documented and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the component source databases (USDA SR Legacy NDB 15152 and NDB 01125 do not provide a combined vitamin D figure applicable to the fried preparation). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water values are estimated from USDA component data and are approximate. The dish is not halal-certified as standard restaurant preparations use butter (dairy) and the halal status of the salted duck egg yolk source is not confirmed. The price of SGD 37.44 from field survey data (See Ya Tonight 今晚有局) reflects a restaurant-grade shared portion. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the finished dish as served. All -1 values reflect nutrients not reported or not reliably estimable from the approved component sources.

Estimated nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy (cooked shrimp NDB 15152) and USDA Foundation Foods (egg yolk NDB 01125) with documented deep-frying fat absorption methodology.
Assigned GI of 45 (low) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database battered fried seafood entries and Atkinson et al. (2021).
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes, specifically vitamin_d_mcg.
